Title: Say No to Palantir in Europe
Description: A powerful US spy-tech company run by billionaires is linked to genocide in Gaza, helps ICE separate families, and fuels Trump’s war with Iran. And now it’s quietly expanding across Europe gaining access to our data, our hospitals, and our police systems.It’s time to expose it and stop its expansion.

Title: Nitrile and latex gloves may cause overestimation of microplastics, U-M study reveals
Description: Nitrile and latex gloves that scientists wear while they are measuring microplastics may lead to a potential overestimation of the tiny pollutants, according to a University of Michigan study.

Title: CSS is DOOMed!
Description: Every wall, floor, barrel, and imp is a div — DOOM rendered entirely in CSS. Using 3D transforms, CSS math functions, @property, clip-path, anchor positioning, and SVG filters to build a fully playable 3D first-person shooter in the browser without Canvas or WebGL.

Title: How I accidentally made the fastest C# CSV parser
Mockup 147 — The Editorial Engine
Description: Editorial pretext demo: animated orbs, live multi-column text flow, and zero DOM measurements.

Title: I Beat the Benchmark and Still Failed
Description: I recently learned a harsh lesson about building startups. You can build a product that beats your competitors in both quality and addictiveness, and still fail miserably if you don't know how to distribute it.

Title: When All You Can Do Is All or Nothing, Do Nothing – CSS Wizardry
Description: If your design system can only apply `loading=lazy` or `fetchpriority=high` blindly, it may be safer not to apply them at all.

Title: I accidentally spammed a year of calendar invites
Description: A calendar sync went sideways, people across my past and future started getting meeting invites from me again, and I spent the afternoon replacing the whole setup with a safer read-only tool.

Title: Building an E2E Encrypted Chat Application with LanceDB and libsodium
How I Built a Public RAG App for Chicago Budget Documents
Description: A practical build log of creating a public-facing RAG app over large municipal budget PDFs, with source citations, embedded document viewing, quality tuning, and production deployment.

Title: How the (Em)ighty Have Fallen—
Description: A journey of the em dash, my obsession with it growing up, my deep appreciation, and the modern constraints upon its usage.

Title: Your Data Never Dies
Description: The purpose fades. Your data doesn't.

Title: Everyone is building a software factory - exe.dev blog
Description: Is exe.dev the compute primitive you've been waiting for?

Title: Tell the Model What to Do, Not What to Avoid
Description: Blind A/B tests on Go code generation showed positive framing in agent instructions consistently outperforms prohibition language. I built a linter to enforce it.

Title: The Revenge of the Data Scientist – Hamel’s Blog - Hamel Husain
